title: KISS SetHardware response loses MSB on wire
description: lichen/subsys/lichen/transport/kiss_transport.c:264-267. spec/kiss-framing.md §4.4: SetHardware response = echo command byte with MSB set (cmd | 0x80). Code computes resp_cmd = KISS_CMD_SETHARDWARE | 0x80 = 0x86 but passes it to kiss_encode(), which builds the wire byte via KISS_CMD_MAKE(port,cmd) = ((port&0xF)<<4)|(cmd&0xF) — the & 0x0F strips the MSB, so 0x06 goes on the wire, indistinguishable from a request. Verified by reviewer + lead. No test covers SetHardware. Fix: emit the full byte 0x86 via a raw-cmd encode path, echo the request's actual port, add a test.
